content: Biomarker testing guides immunotherapy use. PD-L1 expression measured by IHC (22C3, 28-8, SP142 assays); thresholds vary by cancer (≥1%, ≥50%). MSI-H/dMMR detected by PCR or IHC for mismatch repair proteins; predicts response across tumor types. Tumor mutational burden (TMB) ≥10 mutations/Mb correlates with response (FDA-approved for pembrolizumab). Gene expression profiling (inflamed vs cold tumors) investigational. Immune-related adverse events (irAEs) require monitoring: dermatitis (most common), colitis (diarrhea), hepatitis (elevated LFTs), pneumonitis (cough, dyspnea), endocrinopathies (thyroiditis, hypophysitis, adrenal insufficiency). Grading per CTCAE; grade 2 holds treatment + steroids; grade 3-4 permanently discontinues most agents + high-dose steroids (prednisone 1-2 mg/kg). Baseline labs: CBC, CMP, TSH, cortisol; monitor every 3-6 weeks. Contraindications: active autoimmune disease requiring immunosuppression, organ transplant recipients.
